Name: Seigneur Julien de Rochefort
Family:
Wife: Seigneuresse Madeleine, a former beauty now devoted to religious pursuits.
Children: Three daughters (Anne, 28; Brigitte, 26; Claudette, 22), all married strategically to other noble families.
Parents: Both long deceased, left him a considerable fortune and a renowned name.
Extended Family: Nephews and nieces who often seek his counsel and financial support.
Occupation: Seigneur and a well-respected landowner. Known for his shrewd management of his estates and investments.
Personality:
Outwardly: Stern, no-nonsense, and often seen as unapproachable.
Inwardly: Deeply devoted to his family's legacy, though somewhat regretful of not pursuing a more adventurous life.
Social Status: Highly respected for his wealth, wisdom, and lineage. His counsel is sought after by many, including those at court.
Potential Illness: Battling a chronic lung condition, which he keeps hidden to avoid showing weakness.
Body Condition: Robust and tall, with a commanding presence. His once-dark hair now silver, adding to his distinguished appearance.
Hobbies and Interests:
Keen collector of historical manuscripts and relics.
Enjoys breeding horses, known for having some of the finest steeds in the region.
A patron of local monasteries and churches, often involved in philanthropic endeavors.
Finds solace in long walks on his vast estates, a rare moment of peace in his otherwise busy life.
Style of Talking:
Direct and to the point, rarely wastes time on pleasantries.
His voice carries authority and experience, often commanding respect in conversations.
Can be surprisingly warm when discussing topics close to his heart, like his family or hobbies.
Unique Quirks:
Often seen with a distinctive, ancient signet ring, said to belong to his great-grandfather.
Has a ritual of walking through his fields at dawn, claiming it connects him to his land.
Secrets or Scandals:
Rumored to have once been in love with a commoner, a secret he keeps buried.
Allegedly involved in a feud with a neighboring lord, which may have once nearly escalated to bloodshed.
